title: HALEU Fuel Bottleneck for Next-Gen Reactors
catalyst: First commercial SMR deployments (NuScale, Oklo, X-energy, TerraPower) targeting 2028-2030 criticality require High-Assay Low-Enriched Uranium (HALEU) — a fuel grade that today has essentially no Western commercial supply outside Centrus Energy's Piketon facility. DOE's HALEU Availability Program awards in 2024-2025 are flowing through to capex and offtake, but the supply gap through 2030 remains structural.
thesis: Nuclear renaissance coverage focuses on reactor builders, but the binding constraint is fuel. HALEU enrichment is gated by centrifuge capacity, security clearance, and DOE permits — three barriers that prevent a fast supply response even at $10K+/kg-SWU prices. Centrus is the only US-based commercial HALEU producer with an operating cascade, and its expansion timeline aligns with first-fleet SMR fuel loads. Cameco is the broader uranium feedstock proxy as the conversion/enrichment chain tightens. Adjacent to but distinct from our ai-power-bottleneck thesis (GEV/CEG focus on gas turbines and existing nuclear restarts, not new SMR fuel).

source.title: Centrus Energy Corp. — IOWN Screener Report

what_would_promote: Confirmed first commercial HALEU offtake contract with an SMR developer at disclosed pricing, OR Centrus announcing the second cascade expansion timeline with capex committed. Either would convert this from a 'supply story' into a falsifiable revenue catalyst inside the 18-month window required for On Our Radar.
checks_remaining: Need 1 primary source (SEC filing, EIA/DOE data, or company IR) tying HALEU price/volume to a specific name's revenue line. Need to confirm pattern is not duplicative with ai-power-bottleneck — believe it is distinct because mechanism (fuel scarcity) and time horizon (2028-2030 SMR fuel load) differ from gas-turbine grid buildout.

title: On Holding — Insider Cluster Signals Premium Athletic Momentum
catalyst: Three insiders purchased $6.6M of ONON stock in the last 30 days, clustered within a tight window. Open SEC Form 4 filings on a name screening 74 (HOLD) in IOWN — a Growth-sleeve premium athletic brand with double-digit revenue growth, expanding into apparel and lifestyle. No identified news catalyst in the prior 14 days, which raises the quality of the signal per Step 3a heuristics.
thesis: Premium athletic apparel is undergoing share rotation: NKE/UA losing performance-runner mindshare to ONON and HOKA (HOKA-parent Deckers). ONON's gross margin expansion and DTC mix shift have outpaced sell-side estimates for four consecutive quarters. An unprompted insider cluster on a name at $0.74 of fair value (IOWN) suggests management sees runway the market is missing. This is the kind of 'no-news cluster buy' that Step 3 flags as a HIGH-QUALITY signal.

source.title: OpenInsider — ON Holding cluster purchases (3 insiders, $6.6M)

what_would_promote: ONON Q2 2026 earnings (typically mid-August) confirming gross margin trajectory and DTC growth at or above guidance, AND analyst upward revisions across at least 3 sell-side desks. That would convert the insider signal into an On Our Radar-grade catalyst with primary sources beyond the Form 4s themselves.
checks_remaining: Need a falsifiable, dated catalyst (Q2 earnings is implicit but not yet articulated as the thesis trigger). Need a primary source beyond Form 4s — likely the next earnings release. Trade construction undefined.

title: TKO Group — UFC/WWE Rights Cycle Insider Cluster
catalyst: Four TKO insiders purchased a combined $5.5M of stock in the last 30 days, including multiple directors. TKO's UFC media rights with ESPN expire in 2025-2026 and WWE Raw/SmackDown rights with Netflix/USA are entering renewal windows in 2026-2027. Live sports rights pricing has compounded mid-teens per renewal cycle since 2018; both UFC and WWE are scarcity assets in the streaming-fragmented landscape.
thesis: Live sports rights are the most defensible content category as streaming consolidates. UFC has demonstrated 10-15x rights-fee step-ups in prior cycles (Fox → ESPN was ~$1.5B over 7 years). The next UFC US deal — currently in negotiation per multiple trade reports — is widely expected to set a record. A cluster of 4 directors buying ahead of rights-cycle visibility is the kind of insider behavior that historically precedes step-function revenue inflections at media/sports holdcos.

source.title: OpenInsider — TKO Group cluster purchases (4 insiders, $5.5M)

what_would_promote: Public announcement of UFC US rights renewal (or term sheet) at disclosed AAV, OR WWE rights extension with disclosed step-up. Either would convert this from 'pattern recognition' to a dated, falsifiable catalyst with first-party source. Need to triangulate management's capital return policy with the post-renewal cash flow profile.
checks_remaining: No firm catalyst date yet (rights deals announce when they announce). Need ≥1 primary source beyond Form 4s — Endeavor/TKO 10-Q discussion of rights negotiations would suffice. Trade construction undefined.
